error.regex: (cursor.*not found|CursorNotFound|cursor id.*not found|getMore.*cursor.*not found|cursor has been killed)

verdict.summary: Server-side cursor expired (default 10 min timeout) or was killed. Common when iterating large result sets slowly.

dead_ends.why_fails: Cursors with no timeout are never cleaned up if the client crashes. Leaks memory and resources on the server.
Long-lived cursors hold resources and pin old data in WiredTiger cache. Process data faster instead.

workarounds.action: Use batched processing with skip/limit or range queries
workarounds.how: Process in pages: db.collection.find({ _id: { $gt: lastId } }).limit(1000)  # keyset pagination, no cursor timeout risk

workarounds.action: Use aggregation with $merge or $out for large data processing
Increase batchSize to reduce round trips if processing is fast
workarounds.how: db.collection.find().batchSize(5000)  # fewer getMore calls means less time for cursor to expire
